primary_accounting_issue: Détermination, en IFRS 9, de la signification du critère de flux contractuels composés uniquement de principal et d’intérêts (« SPPI ») pour la classification ultérieure d’un actif financier

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the precedence rule that an IFRS standard governs over an IAS standard when addressing the same classification lineage; IFRS 9 directly governs the classification of financial assets and the SPPI test, while IAS 32 only provides background definitions.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty because IFRS 9 expressly sets the classification model and defines principal and interest for this issue.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern the SPPI assessment and the resulting financial asset classification categories.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs support the analysis by defining a financial asset and the contractual nature of financial instrument rights and obligations.

peripheral_authority.reason: IAS 7 addresses cash equivalents and cash flow presentation, not the SPPI classification test for financial assets.
IFRIC 12 refers to IFRS 9 for financial assets in service concession arrangements but does not add peer classification approaches for the SPPI issue itself.
IFRS 3 discusses classification at acquisition date and contingent consideration, which is not needed to identify peer approaches for the SPPI question.

treatment_families.family: Subsequent measurement classification of financial assets based on business model and contractual cash flow characteristics
Interpretation of whether contractual cash flows are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount outstanding

approaches.label: Amortised cost
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies amortised cost as a top-level subsequent measurement category when the business model is to hold to collect and the cash flows are solely payments of principal and interest.

approaches.label: Fair value through other comprehensive income
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value through other comprehensive income as a top-level subsequent measurement category when the business model is both collecting contractual cash flows and selling, and the cash flows are solely payments of principal and interest.

approaches.label: Fair value through profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value through profit or loss as the residual top-level category when the asset does not fall into amortised cost or fair value through other comprehensive income.
